resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def minimumLength(self, s: str) -> int:
        i, j = 0, len(s) - 1
        while i < j and s[i] == s[j]:
            c = s[i]
            while i < j and s[i] == c:
                i += 1
            while i < j and s[j] == c:
                j -= 1
        return j - i + 1
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses two pointers, i and j, to find the minimum length of the string after removing the prefix and suffix. It iterates through the string from both ends, removing the characters that are the same as the character at the current pointers. The time complexity is O(n) because each character is visited at most twice, and the space complexity is O(1) because only a constant amount of space is used.
